summary: All 7 tests pass. The fix was in `scheduler.py:55` — changed `window_start.date()` (UTC date) to `window_start.astimezone(tz).date()` (local date in the event's timezone). Late-night events in negative-offset timezones like US/Eastern cross midnight into the next UTC day, so using the UTC date was off by one day when building their scheduled time.
